description_long: This object is a vintage electronic device consisting of a rectangular, black metal box with rounded edges and a prominent rotary dial on its top surface. Attached to the box is a pair of over-ear headphones with a metal headband and circular ear cups, connected by a coiled cable. The box features several small knobs and connectors, as well as a protruding cylindrical component, likely an antenna or tuning element. The headphones have visible wiring and a dual-prong plug, suggesting analog audio connectivity. The overall design is utilitarian, with a glossy finish and visible screws, indicating robust construction. This device appears to be an early radio receiver or communication headset, suitable for use in technical or industrial urban environments.
description: A vintage black metal electronic box with rotary dial and attached over-ear headphones, featuring a coiled cable and dual-prong plug.
description_view_0: The rectangular box and headphones are shown side by side, highlighting the rotary dial and cylindrical component on the box's top.
description_view_1: The front of the box is visible, showing multiple small knobs and the headphones resting behind it.
description_view_2: The box and headphones are separated, with the cable and dual-prong plug clearly visible.
description_view_3: The rear of the box is shown, with the headphones and cable in the foreground and the main control knob visible on the box.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
